summary:
Preterm birth disrupts fetal vascular development and nitrogen oxides' regulatory pathway, creating conditions conducive to impaired endothelium-dependent relaxation. Given these underlying physiological disturbances, elevated ADMA—known to inhibit nitric oxide synthesis—is biologically plausible as a predictive marker for early endothelial dysfunction in individuals born preterm.  

\boxed{A}
formatted_summary:

Given these underlying physiological disturbances, elevated ADMA—known to inhibit nitric oxide synthesis—is biologically plausible as a predictive marker for early endothelial dysfunction in individuals born preterm.  

\boxed{A}. Answer choice: B
